<par>Maru <latinform>tʃɔʔ³¹</latinform> perhaps a word-family variant of forms with <latinform>ɣ-</latinform>.</par>

<par>Maru <latinform>ɣək³¹</latinform> < PLB <reconstruction>*rəy¹</reconstruction> 'water; liquid' (<xref ref="1013">#1013</xref>). Final <reconstruction>*-əy</reconstruction> regularly becomes <latinform>-it</latinform> in the Maru dialect described in Burling 1966. The unreleased final stop evidently sounds more like a velar in the dialect recorded by Dai Qingxia, who transcribes this rhyme as "<latinform>-ək</latinform>".</par>

<par>Maru <latinform>ɣək³¹</latinform> < PLB <reconstruction>*rəy¹</reconstruction> 'water; liquid' (<xref ref="1013">#1013</xref>). Final <reconstruction>*-əy</reconstruction> regularly becomes <latinform>-it</latinform> in the Maru dialect described in Burling 1966. The unreleased final stop evidently sounds more like a velar in the dialect recorded by Dai Qingxia, who transcribes this rhyme as "<latinform>-ək</latinform>".</par>
<par>Note the secondary final velar, the regular Maru reflex of the rhyme <reconstruction>*-əw</reconstruction>. See note under <xref ref="253">#253</xref>.</par>
